user_message: Can you extract the QBR preparation from this support ticket for Parnell Aerospace? This is a renewal account with declining consumption.

input_artifacts.content:
From: Christopher Martinez <christopher.martinez@parnellaeros.com>
To: Ahmed Hassan <ahmed.hassan@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-03-17 16:04 EST
Subject: Re: QBR preparation Discussion - Next Steps

Thank you for the discussion last week. We'd like to move forward with QBR preparation. Can we schedule a call this week to finalize the timeline?

---
From: Ahmed Hassan <ahmed.hassan@microsoft.com>
To: Christopher Martinez <christopher.martinez@parnellaeros.com>
Date: 2024-02-05 16:08 EST
Subject: QBR preparation - Action Required

Just checking in on the QBR preparation proposal. Our VP Operations is asking for a status update before the board meeting next week.

---
From: Christopher Martinez <christopher.martinez@parnellaeros.com>
To: Ahmed Hassan <ahmed.hassan@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-01-08 12:47 EST
Subject: Follow-up: QBR preparation Review

Just checking in on the QBR preparation proposal. Our VP Operations is asking for a status update before the board meeting next week.

signals.detail: Signal detected for Parnell Aerospace: the current vendor contract has a 90-day exit clause we need to plan around

usability_anchors: Score 5: Output correctly avoids the FM-WF42-B trap (Creates opportunity proposal without sufficient signal); produces complete Signal assessment, opportunity hypothesis with confidence, ownership routing, response draft, next-s
Score 3: Output partially addresses the scenario but shows signs of the FM-WF42-B failure pattern
Score 1: Output exhibits the full FM-WF42-B failure mode (Creates opportunity proposal without sufficient signal)
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct QBR preparation decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
Target trap: FM-WF42-B -- Creates opportunity proposal without sufficient signal
